skill.name: receiving-code-review
skill.description: Guides assistants through code review feedback with verification, clarification, technical pushback, and focused implementation.
skill.summary: Evaluate code review feedback before acting, then implement verified changes with clear technical responses.
skill.author: Cycleaddict

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are false positives caused by Markdown fenced examples, not executable code. Static weak cryptography and network reconnaissance findings are also false positives from prose. The skill does include legitimate workflow instructions that may cause repository inspection and GitHub API replies, so publication is acceptable with a medium operational warning.

medium_findings.title: Operational GitHub API Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents replying to GitHub inline review comments to use the GitHub API reply endpoint. This is a legitimate code review workflow, but it can perform network actions and post content to a repository if the agent has GitHub credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction is explicit and semantically tied to GitHub API use. I found no evidence of exfiltration or malicious endpoints, so the concern is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Codebase Inspection May Invoke Local Search
medium_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to grep the codebase before accepting broad feature work. This is appropriate for code review, but it means the skill may prompt local repository reads or search commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction directly names grep and repository inspection. The behavior is limited to verification and does not show command injection or destructive intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Fences Misidentified as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These blocks contain review workflow pseudocode and example dialogue, not executable scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is Markdown fencing around examples. No shell interpolation, command substitution, or executable Ruby code is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Prose Only
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings occur in natural language examples and metadata. I found no cryptographic algorithm use, hashing API, encryption library, or credential handling in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text or example response text. There is no code path where DES, MD5, SHA1, or another weak algorithm is invoked.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Pattern Is Prose Only
low_findings.description: The static network reconnaissance finding points to guidance about using technical reasoning when responding to review feedback. I found no scanning, port probing, host enumeration, or reconnaissance command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a plain-language instruction inside review guidance. It contains no network target, tool invocation, or discovery workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references grep for verifying whether reviewed code is used. This is a normal developer workflow command, but users should expect local repository search.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command reference is explicit, but it is bounded to codebase verification and not shell execution with untrusted input.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Capable GitHub API Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references the GitHub API endpoint for replying to inline pull request comments. This can create remote side effects when used with authenticated GitHub tooling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint reference is clear and may drive a network request. The endpoint is GitHub-specific and consistent with the skill purpose.

content.value_statement: Code review feedback can be unclear, incomplete, or wrong for the current codebase. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify suggestions, ask focused questions, and implement changes carefully.
actual_capabilities: Structures the response to code review feedback before implementation.
Encourages clarification when review items are incomplete or ambiguous.
Prompts verification against the current codebase before accepting suggestions.
Defines when to push back on technically incorrect or unnecessary feedback.
Guides ordered implementation of blocking, simple, and complex review items.
Provides guidance for replying to GitHub inline review comments.
limitations: Does not perform code review by itself without the user providing feedback.
Cannot know reviewer intent when comments are vague or incomplete.
May require repository access to verify usage, tests, or compatibility.
GitHub replies require authenticated tooling and user approval.

use_cases.title: Clarify Multi-Item Review Feedback
use_cases.description: Pause implementation until unclear review items are restated or clarified.

use_cases.title: Validate External Reviewer Suggestions
use_cases.description: Check whether a requested change fits the codebase before modifying working code.

use_cases.title: Respond to Inline GitHub Comments
use_cases.description: Prepare focused thread replies after verifying the issue and applying the right fix.
use_cases.target_user: Teams using GitHub pull request reviews

prompt_templates.title: Understand Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: I received this code review feedback. Restate each requested change and identify anything unclear before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Reviewer Suggestion
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this reviewer suggestion against the current codebase. Tell me whether it is correct, risky, unnecessary, or unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Review Fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation order for these review comments. Put blocking issues first and list verification steps for each item.

prompt_templates.title: Draft Technical Pushback
prompt_templates.prompt: This review comment may be incorrect. Verify the relevant code and draft a concise technical response with evidence.

output_examples.input: A reviewer says to remove legacy compatibility code.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks supported versions first, then explains whether the legacy path is still required.

output_examples.input: A review contains six items, but two are unclear.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for clarification on the unclear items before starting partial implementation.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks for a large feature that may not be used.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies whether the feature is called and asks whether to remove unused work instead.

best_practices: Provide the full review comment and relevant code context.
Ask the assistant to verify behavior before changing code.
Run tests after each accepted review fix.
anti_patterns: Do not accept every review comment without checking the codebase.
Do not implement partial feedback when related items are unclear.
Do not post GitHub replies before confirming the fix or limitation.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically implement every review comment?
faq.answer: No. It guides verification first, then implementation only when the feedback is clear and technically sound.

faq.question: Can it help with unclear review feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages restating the request and asking focused clarification questions before making changes.

faq.question: Does it support pushback on incorrect feedback?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends concise technical pushback when evidence shows the suggestion is wrong or unnecessary.

faq.question: Will it access my repository?
faq.answer: It may suggest reading or searching the repository to verify feedback. Access depends on the assistant environment.

faq.question: Does it require GitHub?
faq.answer: No. GitHub guidance is included only for inline pull request comment replies.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with AI coding assistants that can inspect code and apply changes.
